signals.headline: Most recent confirmed data:** South Korea's annual inflation rose to **2
signals.raw: Most recent confirmed data:** South Korea's annual inflation rose to **2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in February but below the 2.4% market consensus.[1][2][3] This was driven primarily by rising global oil prices, with transportation costs jumping 5% year-on-year, though government measures like fuel price caps and food vouchers helped contain the increase.[1]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: What analysts anticipated:** The IMF projects South Korea's 2026 inflation at 2
signals.raw:
What analysts anticipated:** The IMF projects South Korea's 2026 inflation at 2.5% for the full year[6], while the Bank of Korea held its policy rate steady at 2.5% at its April meeting, taking a "wait-and-see approach" as it evaluates whether external shocks intensify.[1] Economists warned that sustained fuel prices could push annual inflation above 2.5%.[3]
